text.title: LLM Decision Framework: Requirements and Constraint Checklist
text.summary: Phase-by-phase checklist for systematically evaluating LLM enhancement requirements and constraints before committing to an approach.

rule.statement: Assess data freshness, volume, and sensitivity before evaluating any technical approach; these three factors eliminate at least one or two options before reaching budget or team constraints.
rule.rationale: Daily-changing data eliminates fine-tuning as the primary approach. Fewer than 100 documents eliminates RAG. On-premise data residency eliminates cloud-only managed services. Early elimination saves weeks of work on approaches that were never viable.

list.item: Data freshness: daily/hourly → RAG required; monthly → RAG or periodic fine-tuning; rarely/never → fine-tuning viable
Data volume: <100 documents → prompt engineering with context; 100-10K → RAG; 10K+ → RAG with advanced retrieval
Data sensitivity: public cloud OK → any approach; private cloud only → self-hosted models + RAG; on-premise only → local models, on-prem vector DB
Latency: <500ms → prompt engineering or cached RAG; 500ms-2s → RAG acceptable; >2s → any approach
Accuracy: general quality → prompt engineering; high accuracy with sources → RAG; domain-specific precision → fine-tuning
Citations required: yes → RAG (provides source documents); no → any approach
Output format: flexible → prompt engineering; consistent JSON/XML → structured output; domain-specific format → fine-tuning
Scale: <1K queries/day → any approach; 1K-100K → cost-optimize with caching; >100K → fine-tuned smaller models
Setup budget: <$1K → prompt engineering only; $1K-10K → RAG viable; $10K-50K → fine-tuning viable; >$50K → full stack including RAFT
Monthly budget: <$100 → prompt engineering + caching; $100-1K → basic RAG; $1K-10K → production RAG or fine-tuning
ML expertise: none → prompt engineering, managed services; basic → RAG with existing tools; advanced → fine-tuning
Timeline: days → prompt engineering only; weeks → RAG; months → fine-tuning

rule.statement: Re-evaluate the decision after 3 months in production: LLM provider pricing drops 50-70% annually and the cost-quality frontier shifts regularly.
rule.rationale: A fine-tuning decision made on 2025 pricing may not hold in 2026 when the base model quality improves enough to match the fine-tuned output at standard pricing. Document the decision with current pricing in an ADR for easy future revisit.

text.reference: Architecture Decision Record template for documenting the chosen approach
